content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Toll Free (916) 322-5662 • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
322-5660                322-5901              322-6441                322-6444

December 9, 1982


Donald G. Freeman
Office of the City Attorney
695 Hilby
Seaside, CA  93955

                                                Re: Our No. A-82-189

Dear Mr. Freeman:

     You have asked whether the new Seaside Mayor and a new
city councilmember have potential conflicts of interest
because of the fact that the mayor is employed by the
Monterey Sheriff's Department as a bailiff, and the
councilmember is an investigator in the Public Defender's
office.

     The Political Reform Act prohibits a public official
from making or participating in decisions which may have a
direct financial effect on the official's income, or a
material financial effect on any entity which is a source
of income of $250 or more to the official. Government Code
Sections 87100, 87103,* 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702.
However, a salary from a government agency is not
considered income under the Political Reform Act. Section
82030(b)(2). Therefore the Political Reform Act does not
prohibit an official from participating in decisions
affecting a government agency by which the official is
employed, or from participating in decisions which could
affect the official's government salary. Thus, assuming
that the Public Defender's office, like the Sheriff's
office, is a government agency, the Political Reform Act
does not prohibit either the mayor or the councilmember
from participating in decisions affecting their government
employers or salaries.


*All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise stated.

Donald G. Freeman
December 9, 1982
Page 2


You also asked whether there may be any potential
problems under Section 1090. The Fair Political Practices
Commission is not charged with administering or enforcing
Section 1090. I suggest that you consult the Attorney
General's office concerning both the application of Section
1090 to this situation and the possibility of other
conflicts between the officials' new positions and their
other government employment.

If you have any further questions, I will be happy to
assist you.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660            322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

November 17, 1982


Donald G. Freeman
City Attorney
City of Seaside
695 Hilby
Seaside, CA  93955

Re:  A-82-199

Dear Mr. Freeman:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me for response. If you
have any questions about your advice request, you may
contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

BAM:pin
Enclosure

CITY OF
SEASIDE

P.O. BOX 157, 440 HARCOURT AVE. SEASIDE, CALIFORNIA 93955 TEL. (408) 394-8531

Nov 15 8 52 AM '82.

PLEASE REPLY TO
695 HILBY
SEASIDE CALIF. 93955

November 12, 1982

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ATTORNEY

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: City of Seaside

Gentlemen:

On November 2, 1982, a new mayor and council member were
elected in the City of Seaside. The new mayor is employed by the
County of Monterey as an investigator in the Public Defender's
Office. The councilman is employed by the Monterey County Sheriff's
Department as a bailiff. At present, both are entitled to receive
compensation from the City for their services as elected officials.

The City has several contractual agreements with the County
and shall likely have others in the future. In reference to the
Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the
California Government Code, I would appreciate your assistance
in rendering a legal opinion as to whether either of these persons
may have potential conflicts of interest. It is the desire of
the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to
work on City - County agreements. If they elect to not take the
stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid potential
conflict of interest?

The City will provide you with a listing of current City -
County contracts if this would be helpful to you. Should you need
further information or have any questions, please contact me at
(408) 899-6206.

Any suggestions you can offer will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

DONALD G. FREEMAN

DGF:caj

sections.question: In reference to the Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the California Government Code, whether either of these persons may have potential conflicts of interest. It is the desire of the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to vote on City-County agreements. If they elect to not take the stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid a potential conflict of interest?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a newly elected mayor employed by Monterey County as an investigator and a councilman employed by the Sheriff's Department may have conflicts of interest under Section 1090 when voting on City-County contracts, and whether declining compensation would eliminate such conflicts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has received the request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days, referencing Section 1090 of the Government Code regarding potential conflicts of interest for public officials with concurrent county employment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from City Attorney dated November 12, 1982) and the FPPC's acknowledgment/receipt letter (dated November 17, 1982). The full FPPC advice conclusion is not included in this document - only the transmittal letter confirming receipt and promising response within 21 days. Heavy OCR degradation in some sections reduced confidence slightly.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In reference to the Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the California Government Code, whether either of these persons may have potential conflicts of interest. It is the desire of the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to vote on City-County agreements. If they elect to not take the stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid a potential conflict of interest?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has received the request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days, referencing Section 1090 of the Government Code regarding potential conflicts of interest for public officials with concurrent county employment.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Seaside requests FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts of interest for a newly elected mayor and councilman who are employed by Monterey County, asking whether they can vote on City-County agreements and whether declining compensation would resolve conflicts under the Political Reform Act.
